type=internationalorganization scopes into IO context

heir scoring checks traits

gods optionally reference avatars
court_language modifiers reference culture/language
conditions block uses identical trigger syntax to scripted_triggers
inline formula syntax mirrors scripted_effect parameter blocks; both support $variable$ substitution
on_action effect blocks use scripted effects
on_action trigger and weight_multiplier blocks use scripted triggers
country_interactions block references country interaction keys
vanilla objectives target IO membership and special statuses
both apply modifier fields to scoped entities; differ in activation mechanism (explicit push vs. automatic condition check)

vanilla functions reference policy/law keys (GetOrderOfChivalry etc.)
identical pronoun/tense variant structure and fallback chaining
sort_priority aligns with alert severity values
similar task DAG with prerequisites and completion triggers
levy = yes units are granted by the levies system
maintenance_demand and construction_demand reference production method keys
scripted relations apply opinion/trust biases via opinion_* and trust_* field prefixes
actions block lists generic_action keys from the generic_actions system
join_war_disabled_trigger can inspect war scope which relates to wargoals
possible_production_methods lists named PM keys; unique_production_methods uses inline PM syntax
PMs inside buildings consume and optionally produce specific goods
pop_type field selects which pop type the building employs
estate field restricts building to a specific estate; forbidden_for_estates blocks estate ownership
max_levels, build_time, employment_size reference named script values (e.g. rural_building_cap)
can_end triggers are typically defined as named scripted triggers in disaster_triggers.txt
laws are scoped per gov type via law_gov_group; government_types defines which types exist
government field in reforms refers to gov type keys
heir_selection list in gov type defines which succession methods are valid

estate_preferences block references estate type keys
monthly_towards_* in policy country_modifier blocks push societal value axes
on_activate can call unlock_heir_selection_effect to enable succession methods
PM goods fields list specific good keys as inputs; produced = <good> defines output
identical $argument$ substitution mechanism, parameterized call syntax, and custom_description warning; fully symmetric
levy unit field references a unit type key; unit must be defined in unit_types/
allowed_pop_type and allow (pop-level) filters reference pop type keys
Black Death and Great Pestilence situations link to bubonic_plague and great_pestilence disease keys
international_organization_type associates a situation with an IO (e.g. Papal IO for Reformation)
on_start/on_monthly/on_ending/on_ended use the same effect hooks as on_action
each institution definition declares which age it belongs to via the age field
embrace effects are handled in on_action/ hooks, not in the institution definition
parliament agendas and issues are tied to estate types as participants and agenda owners
IOs (like HRE) have their own parliament type and agenda definitions
parliament issues and laws both define policy motions with accept/reject effects
country rank thresholds interact with government type scale-sensitive mechanics
level field checks current country rank; players can only form countries of higher level
only non-subject great powers (a government scale concept) are eligible for hegemon status
nobles, clergy, and burghers regency definitions gate on estate presence and allow estate-held regencies
select_trigger pipeline is identical — same fields, same target resolution logic
all three systems share the same select_trigger/target-selection pipeline
select_trigger pipeline is identical — same fields, same target resolution logic
international_organization_type field links each resolution to an IO type
proposal_price and price fields reference entries in common/prices/
situations can host resolutions via their resolution system (e.g. Reformation IO resolutions)
stances are assigned to subjects via subject_types or per-country scripted effects
unit category IDs in left/center/right/reserves must match unit_types/unit_categories definitions
quality.category prefix syntax references unit category keys
price_per_unit_distance, construction_demand, and maintenance_demand reference goods prices and demand entries
proximity field counteracts topography proximity penalty; movement_cost stacks with topography movement_cost
free_building_levels in rank_modifier grants additional building slots; building availability depends on rank
construction_demand references goods_demand entries for upgrade costs
building type IDs in setup presets must match entries in common/building_types/
building levels in setups are capped by location rank at scenario start
climate affects disease spread rates and severity (tropical climates have higher malaria/disease risk)
topography build time modifiers (local_rgo_build_time, local_road_building_time) affect location upgrade speed
ethnicities define probability distributions over gene ranges; genes/ defines the slots, ethnicities/ fills them
culture definitions reference ethnicity IDs to determine character appearance
kill_character effect in character_interactions accepts death_reason = <id> parameter
score benchmarks reference country tags including formable nations associated with historical ages
regencies interact with succession types defined in government_types via heir_selection
